mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank intervention risks, leading to higher equity risk premiums and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with 5d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows no predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE falling below 3.5%) signals wage pressures that force central banks to raise rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates demand-side imbalances, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns in mismatched regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows no predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3% signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in contractionary regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 shows no predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) in a low-unemployment environment (UNRATE < 4%) signals overheating labor markets, prompting rate hikes that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns in overheating regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no predictive power (p > 0.1).
